title: Research funding and fellowships
summary: This Bank of England webpage describes administrative details about research funding programs and fellowships, including the Houblon-Norman and George Fellowships. It provides information on eligibility criteria, application procedures, and fund governance but contains no market-moving or policy-relevant content.
key_points: Houblon-Norman and George Fellowships promote research into financial business institutions and economic conditions affecting them
Fellowships range from one month to one year with annual distributions rarely exceeding £120,000
Awards are per diem allowances for subsistence costs only, not salary replacement
Current fund trustees include Clare Lombardelli (Chair, appointed 2024), Anne Glover (appointed 2019), and Nathanaël Benjamin (appointed 2023)
Applications for 2026/27 are closed; re-opening in autumn 2026 for 2027/28 academic year
Fellowships are open to tenured professors (senior) and postdoctoral researchers at all career stages
why_it_matters: This source is not relevant to market structure or macro catalysts. It is an administrative informational page about research fellowship programs at the Bank of England, containing no economic data, policy signals, or market-moving information.
important_numbers: 20 working days processing time for support letters
